question_title: Which turnout range will the official nationwide turnout in Bulgaria’s 2026 parliamentary election fall into?
summary: Bulgaria's April 19, 2026 parliamentary election is the 8th snap election since 2021, triggered by December 2025 mass protests and government resignation. Historical turnout in 2021-2024 averaged 39.8% (range 33-49%), with 6 of 7 elections below 45%. However, multiple recent surveys (Sova Harris, Trend, ANSA) project significantly higher turnout, with absolute voter projections of 3.0-3.1M translating to ~45-47% actual turnout. Key mobilizing factors include former President Radev entering politics with a new anti-corruption party and protest-driven public engagement. The 45-55% range is the modal estimate, reflecting the tension between the strong below-45% historical base rate and credible evidence of higher mobilization. The April 2021 precedent (49.1% during similar protest dynamics) suggests breaking 50% is achievable but not certain.

factors.description: Historical base rate: 6 of 7 recent elections (2021-2024) had turnout below 45%, with mean 39.8% and range 33-49%. Source: IFES Election Guide.

Multiple recent surveys (Sova Harris Mar 7-12, Trend Feb 12-18, ANSA Mar 20) project significantly higher turnout. Absolute voter projections of ~3.0-3.1M voters imply 45-47% turnout against 6.64M eligible voters.

Former president Radev resigned Jan 2026 and entered politics with new anti-corruption party 'Bulgaria Progressista', leading polls at 25-33%. Genuinely unprecedented dynamic that could mobilize disengaged voters.
December 2025 mass protests over corruption led to Zhelyazkov government resignation. Similar to 2021 protest dynamic that produced the era-high 49.1% turnout.
Voter fatigue from 8th election in 5 years could depress turnout despite positive mobilization signals.
Parliament overrode presidential veto on Electoral Code amendments reducing overseas polling stations in non-EU countries, potentially suppressing diaspora turnout.
Pre-2020 Bulgarian elections regularly saw 50-60% turnout (per Sova Harris survey reference). If political moment is genuinely transformative, return to those levels is possible.

Gap between survey headline percentages (55-59%) and absolute voter projections (45-47%) confirms that stated voting intention overestimates actual turnout. Directional pull toward lower end of survey range.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Bulgaria 2026 Parliamentary Election Turnout**

| Option | Probability |
|---|---|
| Below 45.0% | **35%** |
| 45.0%–54.9% | **43%** |
| 55.0%–64.9% | **19%** |
| 65.0% or above | **3%** |

**Key reasoning:**

- **Historical base rate strongly favors below 45%**: 6 of 7 elections since 2021 fell in this range (mean ~40%)
- **But surveys credibly project higher turnout**: Three recent polls project ~3.0-3.1M voters, which translates to ~45-47% actual turnout — firmly in the 45-55% range
- **Novel political dynamics**: Radev's entry into politics + protest-driven government collapse create stronger mobilization signals than any recent election except April 2021 (49.1%)
- **Countervailing factors**: Voter fatigue from 8th election and reduced overseas polling stations pull toward lower turnout
- **55%+ requires exceptional mobilization** — possible but would exceed anything seen in Bulgaria since pre-2020. 65%+ hasn't occurred since 2001

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option containing the official nationwide turnout percentage for Bulgaria’s 2026 parliamentary election, using the Wikipedia page for the election at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Bulgarian_parliamentary_election.
fine_print: Use the final nationwide turnout figure. If the Wikipedia page is unavailable, incomplete, or internally inconsistent by the resolution time, use Bulgaria’s Central Election Commission turnout result. Resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: Bulgaria Central Election Commission https://www.cik.bg/ ; official results portal https://results.cik.bg/
